file.content:
date: July 19, 2024

behavior_changes:
- area: thread_local
  change: |
    Changes the behavior of the ``SlotImpl`` class destructor. With this change the destructor can be called on any thread.
    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag ``envoy.reloadable_features.allow_slot_destroy_on_worker_threads``
    to ``false``.
- area: ext_proc
  change: |
    Adding support for
    :ref:`route_cache_action <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor.route_cache_action>`.
    It specifies the route action to be taken when an external processor response is received in response to request headers.
- area: http2
  change: |
    Changes the default value of ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_use_oghttp2`` to ``true``. This changes the codec used for HTTP/2
    requests and responses. This behavior can be reverted by setting the feature to ``false``.
- area: http3
  change: |
    Added a happy eyeballs feature to HTTP/3 upstream, where it assuming happy eyeballs sorting results in alternating address
    families will attempt the first v4 and v6 address before giving up on HTTP/3.  This change can be reverted by setting
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.http3_happy_eyeballs`` to ``false``.
- area: http2
  change: |
    Passes HTTP/2 ``DATA`` frames through a different codec API. This behavior can be temporarily disabled by setting the runtime
    feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_use_visitor_for_data`` to ``false``.
- area: runtime
  change: |
    Rejecting invalid YAML. This has been an ``ENVOY_BUG`` linked to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/27434
    for over a year with no hard-blockers so should be Ok. This behavior can be temporarily disabled by setting
    the runtime feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.reject_invalid_yaml`` to ``false`` but the runtime guard must be
    parsed before any invalid YAML.
- area: proxy_protocol
  change: |
    Populate typed metadata by default in proxy protocol listener. Typed metadata can be consumed as
    :ref:`TlvsMetadata type <envoy_v3_api_msg_data.core.v3.TlvsMetadata>`.
    This change can be temporarily disabled by setting the runtime flag
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.use_typed_metadata_in_proxy_protocol_listener`` to ``false``.
- area: composite_filter
  change: |
    Adding support for
    :ref:`sample_percent <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.composite.v3.ExecuteFilterAction.sample_percent>`.
    It specifies the probability of the action execution. If not specified, it is 100%.
- area: golang
  change: |
    Move ``Continue``, ``SendLocalReply`` and ``RecoverPanic`` from ``FilterCallbackHandler`` to ``DecoderFilterCallbacks`` and
    ``EncoderFilterCallbacks``, to support full-duplex processing.
- area: tracing/datadog
  change: |
    Disabled remote configuration by default.
    To enable this feature, use the :ref:`remote_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.trace.v3.DatadogConfig.remote_config>` field.
- area: local_ratelimit
  change: |
    Updated the token bucket implementation to use non-timer based token bucket. The tokens will be refilled when
    the token bucket is accessed and no dependency on the timer.
    This behavior can be temporarily reverted by setting the runtime guard
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.no_timer_based_rate_limit_token_bucket`` to ``false``.

minor_behavior_changes:
- area: ext_proc
  change: |
    Timeout errors in external processor now returns ``504 Gateway Timeout`` to downstream clients.
    The previous behavior was returning ``500 Internal Server Error``.
- area: dfp
  change: |
    Changed dynamic forward proxy so local reply errors include DNS resolution details. This behavior can be temporarily
    disabled by setting the runtime feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.dns_details`` to ``false``.
- area: grpc
  change: |
    Changes in ``AsyncStreamImpl`` and ``GoogleAsyncStreamImpl`` now propagate tracing context headers in bidirectional streams when using
    :ref:`Envoy gRPC client <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.GrpcService.envoy_grpc>` or
    :ref:`Google C++ gRPC client <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.GrpcService.google_grpc>`. Previously, tracing context headers
    were not being set when calling external services such as ext_proc.
- area: http
  change: |
    Fixed host header changes for shadow requests to properly handle ipv6 addresses.
- area: tracers
  change: |
    Set status code for OpenTelemetry tracers (previously unset).
- area: config
  change: |
    Stricter validation of a ``google.protobuf.Duration`` field in a config, rejecting invalid values (where the number
    of years is over 292). This can be temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.strict_duration_validation`` to ``false``.
- area: xds
  change: |
    Updated xDS-TP path naming to better comply with RFC-3986. Encoded resource paths can now include an a colon ``:``,
    instead of ``%3A``. This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.xdstp_path_avoid_colon_encoding`` to ``false``.
- area: udp
  change: |
    Change GRO read buffer to 64kB to avoid ``MSG_TRUNC``. And change the way to limit the number of packets processed per event
    loop to work with GRO. This behavior can be reverted by setting runtime guard
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.udp_socket_apply_aggregated_read_limit`` to ``false``.
- area: statistics
  change: |
    Hot restart statistics like ``hot_restart_epoch`` are only set when hot restart is enabled.
- area: dns
  change: |
    Changes the behavior of the ``getaddrinfo`` DNS resolver so that it treats ``EAI_NODATA`` and ``EAI_NONAME``
    as successful queries with empty results, instead of as DNS failures. This change brings the
    ``getaddrinfo`` behavior in-line with the c-ares resolver behavior. This behavior can be reverted by
    setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.dns_nodata_noname_is_success`` to ``false``.
- area: access_log
  change: |
    The upstream connection address, rather than the upstream host address, will be used for the ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS%``,
    ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_PORT%`` and ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS_WITHOUT_PORT%`` access log format specifiers.
    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.upstream_remote_address_use_connection`` to ``false``.
- area: access_log
  change: |
    The ``%CEL%`` formatter supports call functions.
- area: http
  change: |
    Changing header validation checks in the substitution format utility and CEL code to do RFC-compliant header validation.
    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.consistent_header_validation`` to ``false``.
- area: quic
  change: |
    Cache source/destination address instances in a LRU cache for packet read to improve performance.
    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.quic_upstream_socket_use_address_cache_for_read`` to ``false``.
- area: quic
  change: |
    When a quic connection socket is created, the socket's detected transport protocol will be set to ``quic``.
- area: config
  change: |
    In xDS configuration, the :ref:`AUTO <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.AUTO>` value now means
    :ref:`V3 <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.V3>`. :ref:`AUTO <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.AUTO>`
    is the default value of the enum, so this field may be omitted from all configurations now.
- area: filters
  change: |
    Set ``WWW-Authenticate`` header for ``401`` responses from the Basic Auth filter.
- area: jwt_authn
  change: |
    jwt_authn now validates provider URIs. If the validation is too strict it can temporarily be
    disabled by setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.jwt_authn_validate_uri`` to
    ``false``.
- area: http
  change: |
    Removed runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.refresh_rtt_after_request`` and legacy code path.
- area: http
  change: |
    Changing HTTP/2 semi-colon prefixed headers to being sanitized by Envoy code rather than nghttp2. Should be a functional no-op but
    guarded by ``envoy.reloadable_features.sanitize_http2_headers_without_nghttp2``.
- area: http
  change: |
    Envoy will now proxy ``104`` headers from upstream, though as with ``100`` only the first 1xx response
    headers will be sent. ``104`` headers are designated by IETF's draft-ietf-httpbis-resumable-upload RFC.
    This behavioral can be temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.proxy_104`` to ``false``.
- area: jwt_authn
  change: |
    Changes the behavior of the
    :ref:`forward <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtProvider.forward>`
    config. Previously, the config only removes JWT if set in headers. With this addition, the config can also be
    used to remove JWT set in query parameters. This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.jwt_authn_remove_jwt_from_query_params`` to ``false``.
- area: custom response filter
  change: |
    Prevent the redirect policy from sending ``100`` as the overridden response code as the custom response must
    be complete.
- area: http
  change: |
    Reject messages with chunked transfer encoding with chunk extension containing ``CR`` not followed by ``LF``. This can be
    temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.http1_balsa_disallow_lone_cr_in_chunk_extension``
    to ``false``.
- area: quic
  change: |
    A change in QUICHE has renamed all flags formerly of the form ``quic_reloadable_flag_...`` to ``quiche_reloadable_flag_...``.
    Likewise, for ``quic_restart_flag_...``, now ``quiche_restart_flag_...``. Consequently, all Envoy flags of those forms with an
    ``envoy_quic_...`` prefix have been similarly renamed to ``envoy_quiche_...``.
- area: hashing
  change: |
    Change murmurHash2 hashing on big endian platforms to generate the same values as little endian platforms.
- area: gcp_authn
  change: |
    Use the fixed URL format instead of one from the configuration. This behavior can be disabled by setting runtime flag
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.gcp_authn_use_fixed_url`` to false.

bug_fixes:
- area: admission control
  change: |
    Fixed the thread-local controller's average RPS calculation to be calculated over the full
    lookback window. Previously, the controller would calculate the average RPS over the amount of
    time elapsed since the oldest valid request sample. This change brings the behavior in line with
    the documentation.
- area: outlier detection
  change: |
    Fixed :ref:`successful_active_health_check_uneject_host
    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.successful_active_health_check_uneject_host>`.
    Before, a failed health check could uneject the host if the ``FAILED_ACTIVE_HC`` health flag had not been set.
- area: quic
  change: |
    Applied 2 QUICHE patches for crash bugs in ``QuicSpdyStream`` ``OnDataAvailable()`` and ``OnInitialHeaderComplete()``.
- area: quic
  change: |
    Fixed crash bug when QUIC downstream stream was read closed and then timed out.
- area: tls
  change: |
    Fix a ``RELEASE_ASSERT`` when using :ref:`auto_sni <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.UpstreamHttpProtocolOptions.auto_sni>`
    if the downstream request ``:authority`` was longer than 255 characters.
- area: tracing
  change: |
    Fix an issue where span id is missing from OpenTelemetry access log entries.
- area: ext_authz
  change: |
    Added field
    :ref:`validate_mutations <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.validate_mutations>`,
    which, when set to ``true``, adds header and query parameter mutation validation to the HTTP ext_authz
    filter. If an authz response contains invalid mutations, the filter responds to the downstream
    request with HTTP ``500 Internal Server Error``. If you use ext_authz with an untrusted side stream,
    it's recommended you set this to ``true``.
- area: http
  change: |
    Fix a crash when reloading the HTTP Connection Manager via ECDS.
- area: cares
  change: |
    Upgraded c-ares library to 1.20.1 and added fix to c-ares DNS implementation to additionally check for ``ARES_EREFUSED``,
    ``ARES_ESERVFAIL`` and ``ARES_ENOTIMP`` status. Without this fix, ``DestroyChannelOnRefused`` and
    ``CustomResolverValidAfterChannelDestruction`` unit test will break.
- area: udp
  change: |
    Fixed a bug that would cause Envoy to crash when updates to a pre-existing cluster were made (e.g. ``HostSet`` changes).
- area: ext_authz
  change: |
    Handle ``append_action`` from :ref:`external authorization service <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse>`
    that was ignored.
- area: oauth2
  change: |
    Fixed a bug that would cause Envoy to crash when receiving an Oauth callback while the Oauth upstream is unhealthy
    (e.g. due to DNS issues).
- area: http
  change: |
    Fix BalsaParser resetting state too early, guarded by
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.http1_balsa_delay_reset`` which defaults to ``true``.
- area: ext_authz
  change: |
    Set the SNI value from the requested server name if it isn't available on the connection/socket. This applies when
    ``include_tls_session`` is ``true``. The requested server name is set on a connection when filters such as the TLS
    inspector are used.
- area: oauth
  change: |
    The ID token cookie now expires at the same time the id token itself expires, instead of when the access token expires.
- area: decompression
  change: |
    Fixed a bug where Envoy will go into an endless loop when using the brotli decompressor. If the input stream has
    redundant data, the decompressor will loop forever.
- area: websocket
  change: |
    Only ``101`` is considered a successful response for websocket handshake for HTTP/1.1, and Envoy as a proxy will proxy the response
    header from upstream to downstream and then close the request if other status is received. This behavior can be
    reverted by ``envoy_reloadable_features_check_switch_protocol_websocket_handshake``.
- area: async http client
  change: |
    Added one option to disable the response body buffering for mirror request. Also introduced a 32MB cap for the response
    buffer, which can be changed by the runtime flag ``http.async_response_buffer_limit`` based on the product needs.
- area: ext_authz
  change: |
    Validate http service
    :ref:`path_prefix <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.HttpService.path_prefix>`,
    Valid HTTP service ``path_prefix`` configuration must start with ``/``.
- area: local_ratelimit
  change: |
    Fixed a bug where the local rate limit filter would crash when the
    :ref:`enable_x_ratelimit_headers <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.http.ratelimit.v3.RateLimit>`
    is set to ``DRAFT_VERSION_03`` and a send local reply is triggered before the rate limit filter is executed.
- area: admin
  change: |
    Fixed missing :ref:`additional addresses <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.endpoint.v3.Endpoint.AdditionalAddress>`
    for :ref:`LbEndpoint <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.LbEndpoint.endpoint>` in config dump.
- area: tracing
  change: |
    Added support to configure a static config resource detector for the OpenTelemetry tracer.
- area: http
  change: |
    Fixed a bug where additional :ref:`cookie attributes <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.route.v3.RouteAction.HashPolicy.cookie>`
    are not sent properly to clients.
- area: datadog
  change: |
    Bumped the version of datadog to resolve a crashing bug in earlier versions of the library.
- area: lua
  change: |
    Fixed a bug where the user data will reference a dangling pointer to the Lua state and cause a crash.

removed_config_or_runtime:
- area: tls
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.enable_intermediate_ca`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
- area: oauth
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.oauth_use_standard_max_age_value`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
- area: http
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.use_cluster_cache_for_alt_protocols_filter`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
- area: http
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.restart_features.send_goaway_for_premature_rst_streams`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: load_balancing
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.enable_zone_routing_different_zone_counts`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: load_balancing
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.locality_routing_use_new_routing_logic`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: http
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.proxy_status_upstream_request_timeout`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
- area: http

    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.handle_uppercase_scheme`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: tcp
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.detect_and_raise_rst_tcp_connection`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: tls
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.no_full_scan_certs_on_sni_mismatch`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
- area: http
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.http_allow_partial_urls_in_referer`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: oauth
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.oauth_make_token_cookie_httponly`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: http
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.lowercase_scheme`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: oauth
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.hmac_base64_encoding_only`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: upstream
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.convert_legacy_lb_config`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: thrift
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.thrift_connection_draining`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: thrift
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.thrift_allow_negative_field_ids`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: router
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.copy_response_code_to_downstream_stream_info`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: http2
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_decode_metadata_with_quiche`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: ext_authz
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.ext_authz_http_send_original_xff`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: jwt
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.token_passed_entirely`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: outlier detection
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.check_mep_on_first_eject`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: http
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.stop_decode_metadata_on_local_reply`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: http
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.enable_connect_udp_support`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
- area: oauth2
  change: |
    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.oauth_use_url_encoding`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.

new_features:
- area: redis
  change: |
    Added support for all Bloom 1.0.0 commands.
- area: redis
  change: |
    Added support for ``xack``, ``xadd``, ``xautoclaim``, ``xclaim``, ``xdel``, ``xlen``, ``xpending``,
    ``xrange``, ``xrevrange``, ``xtrim``.
- area: hot_restart
  change: |
    Added new command-line flag :option:`--skip-hot-restart-parent-stats`.
- area: matching
  change: |
    Added :ref:`Filter State Input <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.matching.common_inputs.network.v3.FilterStateInput>`
    for matching HTTP input based on filter state objects.
- area: ext_authz
  change: |
    Added :ref:`disallowed_headers <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.disallowed_headers>`
    to specify headers that should never be sent to the external authentication service. Overrides
    :ref:`allowed_headers <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.allowed_headers>`
    if a header matches both.
- area: upstream
  change: |
    Added a new field to ``LocalityLbEndpoints``, :ref:`LocalityLbEndpoints.Metadata
    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.LocalityLbEndpoints.metadata>`, that may be used for transport socket
    matching groups of endpoints.
- area: quic
  change: |
    Added support for QUIC server preferred address when there is a DNAT between the client and Envoy. See
    :ref:`new config
    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.quic.server_preferred_address.v3.FixedServerPreferredAddressConfig.AddressFamilyConfig.dnat_address>`.
- area: cares
  change: |
    Added :ref:`udp_max_queries<envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.network.dns_resolver.cares.v3.CaresDnsResolverConfig.udp_max_queries>`
    option to limit the number of UDP queries.
- area: http
  change: |
    Added :ref:`disable_shadow_host_suffix_append
    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.RouteAction.RequestMirrorPolicy.disable_shadow_host_suffix_append>`
    in :ref:`request_mirror_policies <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.RouteAction.request_mirror_policies>`
    for disabling appending of the ``-shadow`` suffix to the shadowed host/authority header.
- area: http
  change: |
    Added field :ref:`match_upstream <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.SchemeHeaderTransformation.match_upstream>`,
    which, when set to ``true``, will set the downstream request ``:scheme`` to match the upstream transport protocol.
- area: redis
  change: |
    Added support for `inline commands <https://redis.io/docs/reference/protocol-spec/#inline-commands>`_.
- area: proxy_protocol
  change: |
    Added field :ref:`stat_prefix <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.listener.proxy_protocol.v3.ProxyProtocol.stat_prefix>`
    to the proxy protocol listener filter configuration, allowing for differentiating statistics when multiple proxy
    protocol listener filters are configured.
- area: aws_lambda
  change: |
    The ``aws_lambda`` filter now supports the
    :ref:`credentials <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.aws_lambda.v3.Config.credentials>` parameter.
    This enables setting AWS credentials from the filter configuration.
- area: access_log
  change: |
    Added support for :ref:`%UPSTREAM_HOST_NAME% <config_access_log_format_upstream_host_name>` for the upstream host
    identifier.
- area: access_loggers
  change: |
    Added ``TRACE_ID`` :ref:`access log formatter <config_access_log_format>`.
- area: healthcheck
  change: |
    Added support to healthcheck with ProxyProtocol in TCP Healthcheck by setting
    :ref:`health_check_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.TcpHealthCheck.proxy_protocol_config>`.
- area: local_rate_limit
  change: |
    Added support for :ref:`local cluster rate limit
    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.local_ratelimit.v3.LocalRateLimit.local_cluster_rate_limit>`.
    If set, the token buckets of the local rate limit will be shared across all the Envoy instances in the local
    cluster.
- area: ext_authz
  change: |
    Added
    :ref:`decoder_header_mutation_rules <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.decoder_header_mutation_rules>`
    which allows you to configure what decoder header mutations are allowed from the ext_authz
    service as well as whether to fail the downstream request if disallowed mutations are requested.
- area: access_log
  change: |
    added new ``access_log`` command operators to retrieve upstream connection information change: ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_URI_SAN%``,
    ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_IP_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_DNS_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_URI_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_DNS_SAN%``,
    ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_IP_SAN%``.
- area: wasm
  change: |
    Update WASM filter to support use as an upstream filter.
- area: open_telemetry
  change: |
    Added :ref:`stat_prefix
    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.access_loggers.open_telemetry.v3.OpenTelemetryAccessLogConfig.stat_prefix>`
    configuration to support additional stat prefix for the OpenTelemetry logger.
- area: thrift
  change: |
    Added implementation of :ref:`thrift to metadata <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.http.thrift_to_metadata.v3.ThriftToMetadata>`
    HTTP filter.
- area: open_telemetry
  change: |
    Added :ref:`formatters
    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.access_loggers.open_telemetry.v3.OpenTelemetryAccessLogConfig.formatters>`
    configuration to support extension formatter for the OpenTelemetry logger.
- area: routing
  change: |
    Added support in :ref:`file datasource <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.DirectResponseAction.body>` implementation
    to listen to file changes and dynamically update the response when :ref:`watched_directory
    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.datasource.watched_directory>`
    is configured in :ref:`DataSource <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.core.v3.datasource>`.
- area: outlier detection
  change: |
    Added :ref:`always_eject_one_host<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.always_eject_one_host>`
    to optionally override the :ref:`max_ejection_percent<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.max_ejection_percent>`.
- area: listener
  change: |
    Added :ref:`bypass_overload_manager <envoy_v3_api_field_config.listener.v3.Listener.bypass_overload_manager>`
    to bypass the overload manager for a listener. When set to ``true``, the listener will not be subject to overload protection.
- area: ext_authz
  change: |
    Added
    :ref:`enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion
    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion>`,
    which allows ext_authz to be configured to ignore dynamic metadata in ext_authz responses.
- area: rbac
  change: |
    The RBAC filter will now log the enforced rule to the dynamic metadata field
    ``enforced_effective_policy_id`` and the result to the dynamic metadata field
    ``enforced_engine_result``. These are only populated if a non-shadow engine exists.
- area: jwt_authn
  change: |
    Added :ref:`strip_failure_response
    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtAuthentication.strip_failure_response>`
    to allow stripping the failure response details from the JWT authentication filter.
- area: formatter
  change: |
    Added formatters for :ref:`METADATA(VIRTUAL_HOST)<envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.formatter.metadata.v3.Metadata>`.
- area: quic
  change: |
    Added new interface to ``QuicListenerFilter`` that is called on receiving the first packet.
- area: quic
  change: |
    Added :ref:`DataSourceServerPreferredAddressConfig
    <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.quic.server_preferred_address.v3.DataSourceServerPreferredAddressConfig>` for cases when
    the control plane does not know the correct configuration for the server preferred address.
- area: tls
  change: |
    Added support to match against ``OtherName`` SAN-type under :ref:`match_typed_subject_alt_names
    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.transport_sockets.tls.v3.CertificateValidationContext.match_typed_subject_alt_names>`.
    An additional field ``oid`` is added to :ref:`SubjectAltNameMatcher
    <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.transport_sockets.tls.v3.SubjectAltNameMatcher>` to support this change.
- area: retry
  change: |
    Added :ref:`reset-before-request retry policy <config_http_filters_router_x-envoy-retry-on>`.
- area: ext_proc
  change: |
    Added support for observability mode which deprecates ``async_mode``. If enabled, each part of the HTTP request or response
    specified by ``ProcessingMode`` is sent without waiting for the response from the ext_proc service. It is "Send and Go" mode
    that can be used by external processor to observe Envoy data and status.
- area: grpc
  change: |
    Added support for flow control in Envoy gRPC side stream. This behavior can be disabled by setting the runtime flag
    ``envoy.reloadable_features.grpc_side_stream_flow_control`` to ``false``.
- area: oauth
  change: |
    Added :ref:`disable_id_token_set_cookie <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.oauth2.v3.OAuth2Config.disable_id_token_set_cookie>`
    to disable setting the ID Token cookie.
- area: dns_filter
  change: |
    Added support for wildcard resolution in :ref:`inline_dns_table
    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.udp.dns_filter.v3.DnsFilterConfig.ServerContextConfig.inline_dns_table>`
    when DNS filter is working in server mode.
- area: quic
  change: |
    QUIC stream reset error code will be added to transport failure reason.
    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag ``envoy.reloadable_features.report_stream_reset_error_code``
    to ``false``.

deprecated:
- area: tracing
  change: |
    Disable OpenCensus by default, as it is
    `no longer supported/maintained upstream <https://opentelemetry.io/blog/2023/sunsetting-opencensus/>`_.
    This extension can be replaced with the OpenTelemetry tracer and collector.

1ŌåÆdate: July 19, 2024
     2ŌåÆ
     3ŌåÆbehavior_changes:
     4ŌåÆ- area: thread_local
     5ŌåÆ  change: |
     6ŌåÆ    Changes the behavior of the ``SlotImpl`` class destructor. With this change the destructor can be called on any thread.
     7ŌåÆ    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag ``envoy.reloadable_features.allow_slot_destroy_on_worker_threads``
     8ŌåÆ    to ``false``.
     9ŌåÆ- area: ext_proc
    10ŌåÆ  change: |
    11ŌåÆ    Adding support for
    12ŌåÆ    :ref:`route_cache_action <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor.route_cache_action>`.
    13ŌåÆ    It specifies the route action to be taken when an external processor response is received in response to request headers.
    14ŌåÆ- area: http2
    15ŌåÆ  change: |
    16ŌåÆ    Changes the default value of ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_use_oghttp2`` to ``true``. This changes the codec used for HTTP/2
    17ŌåÆ    requests and responses. This behavior can be reverted by setting the feature to ``false``.
    18ŌåÆ- area: http3
    19ŌåÆ  change: |
    20ŌåÆ    Added a happy eyeballs feature to HTTP/3 upstream, where it assuming happy eyeballs sorting results in alternating address
    21ŌåÆ    families will attempt the first v4 and v6 address before giving up on HTTP/3.  This change can be reverted by setting
    22ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.http3_happy_eyeballs`` to ``false``.
    23ŌåÆ- area: http2
    24ŌåÆ  change: |
    25ŌåÆ    Passes HTTP/2 ``DATA`` frames through a different codec API. This behavior can be temporarily disabled by setting the runtime
    26ŌåÆ    feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_use_visitor_for_data`` to ``false``.
    27ŌåÆ- area: runtime
    28ŌåÆ  change: |
    29ŌåÆ    Rejecting invalid YAML. This has been an ``ENVOY_BUG`` linked to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/27434
    30ŌåÆ    for over a year with no hard-blockers so should be Ok. This behavior can be temporarily disabled by setting
    31ŌåÆ    the runtime feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.reject_invalid_yaml`` to ``false`` but the runtime guard must be
    32ŌåÆ    parsed before any invalid YAML.
    33ŌåÆ- area: proxy_protocol
    34ŌåÆ  change: |
    35ŌåÆ    Populate typed metadata by default in proxy protocol listener. Typed metadata can be consumed as
    36ŌåÆ    :ref:`TlvsMetadata type <envoy_v3_api_msg_data.core.v3.TlvsMetadata>`.
    37ŌåÆ    This change can be temporarily disabled by setting the runtime flag
    38ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.use_typed_metadata_in_proxy_protocol_listener`` to ``false``.
    39ŌåÆ- area: composite_filter
    40ŌåÆ  change: |
    41ŌåÆ    Adding support for
    42ŌåÆ    :ref:`sample_percent <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.composite.v3.ExecuteFilterAction.sample_percent>`.
    43ŌåÆ    It specifies the probability of the action execution. If not specified, it is 100%.
    44ŌåÆ- area: golang
    45ŌåÆ  change: |
    46ŌåÆ    Move ``Continue``, ``SendLocalReply`` and ``RecoverPanic`` from ``FilterCallbackHandler`` to ``DecoderFilterCallbacks`` and
    47ŌåÆ    ``EncoderFilterCallbacks``, to support full-duplex processing.
    48ŌåÆ- area: tracing/datadog
    49ŌåÆ  change: |
    50ŌåÆ    Disabled remote configuration by default.
    51ŌåÆ    To enable this feature, use the :ref:`remote_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.trace.v3.DatadogConfig.remote_config>` field.
    52ŌåÆ- area: local_ratelimit
    53ŌåÆ  change: |
    54ŌåÆ    Updated the token bucket implementation to use non-timer based token bucket. The tokens will be refilled when
    55ŌåÆ    the token bucket is accessed and no dependency on the timer.
    56ŌåÆ    This behavior can be temporarily reverted by setting the runtime guard
    57ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.no_timer_based_rate_limit_token_bucket`` to ``false``.
    58ŌåÆ
    59ŌåÆminor_behavior_changes:
    60ŌåÆ- area: ext_proc
    61ŌåÆ  change: |
    62ŌåÆ    Timeout errors in external processor now returns ``504 Gateway Timeout`` to downstream clients.
    63ŌåÆ    The previous behavior was returning ``500 Internal Server Error``.
    64ŌåÆ- area: dfp
    65ŌåÆ  change: |
    66ŌåÆ    Changed dynamic forward proxy so local reply errors include DNS resolution details. This behavior can be temporarily
    67ŌåÆ    disabled by setting the runtime feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.dns_details`` to ``false``.
    68ŌåÆ- area: grpc
    69ŌåÆ  change: |
    70ŌåÆ    Changes in ``AsyncStreamImpl`` and ``GoogleAsyncStreamImpl`` now propagate tracing context headers in bidirectional streams when using
    71ŌåÆ    :ref:`Envoy gRPC client <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.GrpcService.envoy_grpc>` or
    72ŌåÆ    :ref:`Google C++ gRPC client <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.GrpcService.google_grpc>`. Previously, tracing context headers
    73ŌåÆ    were not being set when calling external services such as ext_proc.
    74ŌåÆ- area: http
    75ŌåÆ  change: |
    76ŌåÆ    Fixed host header changes for shadow requests to properly handle ipv6 addresses.
    77ŌåÆ- area: tracers
    78ŌåÆ  change: |
    79ŌåÆ    Set status code for OpenTelemetry tracers (previously unset).
    80ŌåÆ- area: config
    81ŌåÆ  change: |
    82ŌåÆ    Stricter validation of a ``google.protobuf.Duration`` field in a config, rejecting invalid values (where the number
    83ŌåÆ    of years is over 292). This can be temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard
    84ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.strict_duration_validation`` to ``false``.
    85ŌåÆ- area: xds
    86ŌåÆ  change: |
    87ŌåÆ    Updated xDS-TP path naming to better comply with RFC-3986. Encoded resource paths can now include an a colon ``:``,
    88ŌåÆ    instead of ``%3A``. This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag
    89ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.xdstp_path_avoid_colon_encoding`` to ``false``.
    90ŌåÆ- area: udp
    91ŌåÆ  change: |
    92ŌåÆ    Change GRO read buffer to 64kB to avoid ``MSG_TRUNC``. And change the way to limit the number of packets processed per event
    93ŌåÆ    loop to work with GRO. This behavior can be reverted by setting runtime guard
    94ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.udp_socket_apply_aggregated_read_limit`` to ``false``.
    95ŌåÆ- area: statistics
    96ŌåÆ  change: |
    97ŌåÆ    Hot restart statistics like ``hot_restart_epoch`` are only set when hot restart is enabled.
    98ŌåÆ- area: dns
    99ŌåÆ  change: |
   100ŌåÆ    Changes the behavior of the ``getaddrinfo`` DNS resolver so that it treats ``EAI_NODATA`` and ``EAI_NONAME``
   101ŌåÆ    as successful queries with empty results, instead of as DNS failures. This change brings the
   102ŌåÆ    ``getaddrinfo`` behavior in-line with the c-ares resolver behavior. This behavior can be reverted by
   103ŌåÆ    setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.dns_nodata_noname_is_success`` to ``false``.
   104ŌåÆ- area: access_log
   105ŌåÆ  change: |
   106ŌåÆ    The upstream connection address, rather than the upstream host address, will be used for the ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS%``,
   107ŌåÆ    ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_PORT%`` and ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS_WITHOUT_PORT%`` access log format specifiers.
   108ŌåÆ    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
   109ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.upstream_remote_address_use_connection`` to ``false``.
   110ŌåÆ- area: access_log
   111ŌåÆ  change: |
   112ŌåÆ    The ``%CEL%`` formatter supports call functions.
   113ŌåÆ- area: http
   114ŌåÆ  change: |
   115ŌåÆ    Changing header validation checks in the substitution format utility and CEL code to do RFC-compliant header validation.
   116ŌåÆ    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.consistent_header_validation`` to ``false``.
   117ŌåÆ- area: quic
   118ŌåÆ  change: |
   119ŌåÆ    Cache source/destination address instances in a LRU cache for packet read to improve performance.
   120ŌåÆ    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
   121ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.quic_upstream_socket_use_address_cache_for_read`` to ``false``.
   122ŌåÆ- area: quic
   123ŌåÆ  change: |
   124ŌåÆ    When a quic connection socket is created, the socket's detected transport protocol will be set to ``quic``.
   125ŌåÆ- area: config
   126ŌåÆ  change: |
   127ŌåÆ    In xDS configuration, the :ref:`AUTO <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.AUTO>` value now means
   128ŌåÆ    :ref:`V3 <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.V3>`. :ref:`AUTO <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.AUTO>`
   129ŌåÆ    is the default value of the enum, so this field may be omitted from all configurations now.
   130ŌåÆ- area: filters
   131ŌåÆ  change: |
   132ŌåÆ    Set ``WWW-Authenticate`` header for ``401`` responses from the Basic Auth filter.
   133ŌåÆ- area: jwt_authn
   134ŌåÆ  change: |
   135ŌåÆ    jwt_authn now validates provider URIs. If the validation is too strict it can temporarily be
   136ŌåÆ    disabled by setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.jwt_authn_validate_uri`` to
   137ŌåÆ    ``false``.
   138ŌåÆ- area: http
   139ŌåÆ  change: |
   140ŌåÆ    Removed runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.refresh_rtt_after_request`` and legacy code path.
   141ŌåÆ- area: http
   142ŌåÆ  change: |
   143ŌåÆ    Changing HTTP/2 semi-colon prefixed headers to being sanitized by Envoy code rather than nghttp2. Should be a functional no-op but
   144ŌåÆ    guarded by ``envoy.reloadable_features.sanitize_http2_headers_without_nghttp2``.
   145ŌåÆ- area: http
   146ŌåÆ  change: |
   147ŌåÆ    Envoy will now proxy ``104`` headers from upstream, though as with ``100`` only the first 1xx response
   148ŌåÆ    headers will be sent. ``104`` headers are designated by IETF's draft-ietf-httpbis-resumable-upload RFC.
   149ŌåÆ    This behavioral can be temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard
   150ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.proxy_104`` to ``false``.
   151ŌåÆ- area: jwt_authn
   152ŌåÆ  change: |
   153ŌåÆ    Changes the behavior of the
   154ŌåÆ    :ref:`forward <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtProvider.forward>`
   155ŌåÆ    config. Previously, the config only removes JWT if set in headers. With this addition, the config can also be
   156ŌåÆ    used to remove JWT set in query parameters. This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
   157ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.jwt_authn_remove_jwt_from_query_params`` to ``false``.
   158ŌåÆ- area: custom response filter
   159ŌåÆ  change: |
   160ŌåÆ    Prevent the redirect policy from sending ``100`` as the overridden response code as the custom response must
   161ŌåÆ    be complete.
   162ŌåÆ- area: http
   163ŌåÆ  change: |
   164ŌåÆ    Reject messages with chunked transfer encoding with chunk extension containing ``CR`` not followed by ``LF``. This can be
   165ŌåÆ    temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.http1_balsa_disallow_lone_cr_in_chunk_extension``
   166ŌåÆ    to ``false``.
   167ŌåÆ- area: quic
   168ŌåÆ  change: |
   169ŌåÆ    A change in QUICHE has renamed all flags formerly of the form ``quic_reloadable_flag_...`` to ``quiche_reloadable_flag_...``.
   170ŌåÆ    Likewise, for ``quic_restart_flag_...``, now ``quiche_restart_flag_...``. Consequently, all Envoy flags of those forms with an
   171ŌåÆ    ``envoy_quic_...`` prefix have been similarly renamed to ``envoy_quiche_...``.
   172ŌåÆ- area: hashing
   173ŌåÆ  change: |
   174ŌåÆ    Change murmurHash2 hashing on big endian platforms to generate the same values as little endian platforms.
   175ŌåÆ- area: gcp_authn
   176ŌåÆ  change: |
   177ŌåÆ    Use the fixed URL format instead of one from the configuration. This behavior can be disabled by setting runtime flag
   178ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.gcp_authn_use_fixed_url`` to false.
   179ŌåÆ
   180ŌåÆbug_fixes:
   181ŌåÆ- area: admission control
   182ŌåÆ  change: |
   183ŌåÆ    Fixed the thread-local controller's average RPS calculation to be calculated over the full
   184ŌåÆ    lookback window. Previously, the controller would calculate the average RPS over the amount of
   185ŌåÆ    time elapsed since the oldest valid request sample. This change brings the behavior in line with
   186ŌåÆ    the documentation.
   187ŌåÆ- area: outlier detection
   188ŌåÆ  change: |
   189ŌåÆ    Fixed :ref:`successful_active_health_check_uneject_host
   190ŌåÆ    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.successful_active_health_check_uneject_host>`.
   191ŌåÆ    Before, a failed health check could uneject the host if the ``FAILED_ACTIVE_HC`` health flag had not been set.
   192ŌåÆ- area: quic
   193ŌåÆ  change: |
   194ŌåÆ    Applied 2 QUICHE patches for crash bugs in ``QuicSpdyStream`` ``OnDataAvailable()`` and ``OnInitialHeaderComplete()``.
   195ŌåÆ- area: quic
   196ŌåÆ  change: |
   197ŌåÆ    Fixed crash bug when QUIC downstream stream was read closed and then timed out.
   198ŌåÆ- area: tls
   199ŌåÆ  change: |
   200ŌåÆ    Fix a ``RELEASE_ASSERT`` when using :ref:`auto_sni <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.UpstreamHttpProtocolOptions.auto_sni>`
   201ŌåÆ    if the downstream request ``:authority`` was longer than 255 characters.
   202ŌåÆ- area: tracing
   203ŌåÆ  change: |
   204ŌåÆ    Fix an issue where span id is missing from OpenTelemetry access log entries.
   205ŌåÆ- area: ext_authz
   206ŌåÆ  change: |
   207ŌåÆ    Added field
   208ŌåÆ    :ref:`validate_mutations <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.validate_mutations>`,
   209ŌåÆ    which, when set to ``true``, adds header and query parameter mutation validation to the HTTP ext_authz
   210ŌåÆ    filter. If an authz response contains invalid mutations, the filter responds to the downstream
   211ŌåÆ    request with HTTP ``500 Internal Server Error``. If you use ext_authz with an untrusted side stream,
   212ŌåÆ    it's recommended you set this to ``true``.
   213ŌåÆ- area: http
   214ŌåÆ  change: |
   215ŌåÆ    Fix a crash when reloading the HTTP Connection Manager via ECDS.
   216ŌåÆ- area: cares
   217ŌåÆ  change: |
   218ŌåÆ    Upgraded c-ares library to 1.20.1 and added fix to c-ares DNS implementation to additionally check for ``ARES_EREFUSED``,
   219ŌåÆ    ``ARES_ESERVFAIL`` and ``ARES_ENOTIMP`` status. Without this fix, ``DestroyChannelOnRefused`` and
   220ŌåÆ    ``CustomResolverValidAfterChannelDestruction`` unit test will break.
   221ŌåÆ- area: udp
   222ŌåÆ  change: |
   223ŌåÆ    Fixed a bug that would cause Envoy to crash when updates to a pre-existing cluster were made (e.g. ``HostSet`` changes).
   224ŌåÆ- area: ext_authz
   225ŌåÆ  change: |
   226ŌåÆ    Handle ``append_action`` from :ref:`external authorization service <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse>`
   227ŌåÆ    that was ignored.
   228ŌåÆ- area: oauth2
   229ŌåÆ  change: |
   230ŌåÆ    Fixed a bug that would cause Envoy to crash when receiving an Oauth callback while the Oauth upstream is unhealthy
   231ŌåÆ    (e.g. due to DNS issues).
   232ŌåÆ- area: http
   233ŌåÆ  change: |
   234ŌåÆ    Fix BalsaParser resetting state too early, guarded by
   235ŌåÆ    ``envoy.reloadable_features.http1_balsa_delay_reset`` which defaults to ``true``.
   236ŌåÆ- area: ext_authz
   237ŌåÆ  change: |
   238ŌåÆ    Set the SNI value from the requested server name if it isn't available on the connection/socket. This applies when
   239ŌåÆ    ``include_tls_session`` is ``true``. The requested server name is set on a connection when filters such as the TLS
   240ŌåÆ    inspector are used.
   241ŌåÆ- area: oauth
   242ŌåÆ  change: |
   243ŌåÆ    The ID token cookie now expires at the same time the id token itself expires, instead of when the access token expires.
   244ŌåÆ- area: decompression
   245ŌåÆ  change: |
   246ŌåÆ    Fixed a bug where Envoy will go into an endless loop when using the brotli decompressor. If the input stream has
   247ŌåÆ    redundant data, the decompressor will loop forever.
   248ŌåÆ- area: websocket
   249ŌåÆ  change: |
   250ŌåÆ    Only ``101`` is considered a successful response for websocket handshake for HTTP/1.1, and Envoy as a proxy will proxy the response
   251ŌåÆ    header from upstream to downstream and then close the request if other status is received. This behavior can be
   252ŌåÆ    reverted by ``envoy_reloadable_features_check_switch_protocol_websocket_handshake``.
   253ŌåÆ- area: async http client
   254ŌåÆ  change: |
   255ŌåÆ    Added one option to disable the response body buffering for mirror request. Also introduced a 32MB cap for the response
   256ŌåÆ    buffer, which can be changed by the runtime flag ``http.async_response_buffer_limit`` based on the product needs.
   257ŌåÆ- area: ext_authz
   258ŌåÆ  change: |
   259ŌåÆ    Validate http service
   260ŌåÆ    :ref:`path_prefix <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.HttpService.path_prefix>`,
   261ŌåÆ    Valid HTTP service ``path_prefix`` configuration must start with ``/``.
   262ŌåÆ- area: local_ratelimit
   263ŌåÆ  change: |
   264ŌåÆ    Fixed a bug where the local rate limit filter would crash when the
   265ŌåÆ    :ref:`enable_x_ratelimit_headers <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.http.ratelimit.v3.RateLimit>`
   266ŌåÆ    is set to ``DRAFT_VERSION_03`` and a send local reply is triggered before the rate limit filter is executed.
   267ŌåÆ- area: admin
   268ŌåÆ  change: |
   269ŌåÆ    Fixed missing :ref:`additional addresses <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.endpoint.v3.Endpoint.AdditionalAddress>`
   270ŌåÆ    for :ref:`LbEndpoint <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.LbEndpoint.endpoint>` in config dump.
   271ŌåÆ- area: tracing
   272ŌåÆ  change: |
   273ŌåÆ    Added support to configure a static config resource detector for the OpenTelemetry tracer.
   274ŌåÆ- area: http
   275ŌåÆ  change: |
   276ŌåÆ    Fixed a bug where additional :ref:`cookie attributes <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.route.v3.RouteAction.HashPolicy.cookie>`
   277ŌåÆ    are not sent properly to clients.
   278ŌåÆ- area: datadog
   279ŌåÆ  change: |
   280ŌåÆ    Bumped the version of datadog to resolve a crashing bug in earlier versions of the library.
   281ŌåÆ- area: lua
   282ŌåÆ  change: |
   283ŌåÆ    Fixed a bug where the user data will reference a dangling pointer to the Lua state and cause a crash.
   284ŌåÆ
   285ŌåÆremoved_config_or_runtime:
   286ŌåÆ- area: tls
   287ŌåÆ  change: |
   288ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.enable_intermediate_ca`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
   289ŌåÆ- area: oauth
   290ŌåÆ  change: |
   291ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.oauth_use_standard_max_age_value`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
   292ŌåÆ- area: http
   293ŌåÆ  change: |
   294ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.use_cluster_cache_for_alt_protocols_filter`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
   295ŌåÆ- area: http
   296ŌåÆ  change: |
   297ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.restart_features.send_goaway_for_premature_rst_streams`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   298ŌåÆ- area: load_balancing
   299ŌåÆ  change: |
   300ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.enable_zone_routing_different_zone_counts`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   301ŌåÆ- area: load_balancing
   302ŌåÆ  change: |
   303ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.locality_routing_use_new_routing_logic`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   304ŌåÆ- area: http
   305ŌåÆ  change: |
   306ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.proxy_status_upstream_request_timeout`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
   307ŌåÆ- area: http
   308ŌåÆ  change: |
   309ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.handle_uppercase_scheme`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   310ŌåÆ- area: tcp
   311ŌåÆ  change: |
   312ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.detect_and_raise_rst_tcp_connection`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   313ŌåÆ- area: tls
   314ŌåÆ  change: |
   315ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.no_full_scan_certs_on_sni_mismatch`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.
   316ŌåÆ- area: http
   317ŌåÆ  change: |
   318ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.http_allow_partial_urls_in_referer`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   319ŌåÆ- area: oauth
   320ŌåÆ  change: |
   321ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.oauth_make_token_cookie_httponly`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   322ŌåÆ- area: http
   323ŌåÆ  change: |
   324ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.lowercase_scheme`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   325ŌåÆ- area: oauth
   326ŌåÆ  change: |
   327ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.hmac_base64_encoding_only`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   328ŌåÆ- area: upstream
   329ŌåÆ  change: |
   330ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.convert_legacy_lb_config`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   331ŌåÆ- area: thrift
   332ŌåÆ  change: |
   333ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.thrift_connection_draining`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   334ŌåÆ- area: thrift
   335ŌåÆ  change: |
   336ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.thrift_allow_negative_field_ids`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   337ŌåÆ- area: router
   338ŌåÆ  change: |
   339ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.copy_response_code_to_downstream_stream_info`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   340ŌåÆ- area: http2
   341ŌåÆ  change: |
   342ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_decode_metadata_with_quiche`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   343ŌåÆ- area: ext_authz
   344ŌåÆ  change: |
   345ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.ext_authz_http_send_original_xff`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.
   346ŌåÆ- area: jwt
   347ŌåÆ  change: |
   348ŌåÆ    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.token_passed_entirely`` runtime flag and legacy code paths.

arguments.content:
# Envoy External Processing Filter Migration Guide: v1.30 ŌåÆ v1.31

**Version Date**: July 2024
**API Version**: v3
**Filter Name**: `envoy.filters.http.ext_proc`

---

## Overview

The External Processing (ext_proc) HTTP filter in Envoy v1.31 introduces several significant enhancements focused on improving route cache management and observability. This migration guide covers the changes from Envoy v1.30.0 (April 2024) to v1.31.0 (July 2024).

**Key Changes Summary:**
- New `route_cache_action` field provides flexible route cache management
- Introduction of `observability_mode` for non-blocking external processor communication
- Support for `deferred_close_timeout` to manage gRPC stream lifecycle in observability mode
- Breaking change: `disable_clear_route_cache` is now promoted into a oneof with `route_cache_action`
- Behavioral change: Timeout errors now return 504 Gateway Timeout instead of 500 Internal Server Error

---

## Breaking Changes

### 1. Route Cache Management Field Promotion

**Impact**: Configurations using `disable_clear_route_cache` with non-default values alongside `route_cache_action` will fail validation.

**What Changed:**
- The `disable_clear_route_cache` (field 11) field is now being promoted into a oneof group called `clear_route_cache_type`
- A new field `route_cache_action` (field 18) is introduced as the preferred way to manage route cache behavior
- Only ONE of these fields can be set to a non-default value

**Proto Definition (v1.31):**

```protobuf
message ExternalProcessor {
  // NEW: RouteCacheAction enum
  enum RouteCacheAction {
    DEFAULT = 0;      // Default behavior - clear cache only when set in response
    CLEAR = 1;        // Always clear route cache
    RETAIN = 2;       // Never clear route cache
  }

  // DEPRECATED (but still functional): Sets route cache action to RETAIN
  bool disable_clear_route_cache = 11
      [(udpa.annotations.field_migrate).oneof_promotion = "clear_route_cache_type"];

  // NEW: Replaces disable_clear_route_cache functionality
  RouteCacheAction route_cache_action = 18
      [(udpa.annotations.field_migrate).oneof_promotion = "clear_route_cache_type"];
}
```

**Migration Steps:**

1. **Identify Current Configuration**:
   ```yaml
   # v1.30 configuration
   ext_proc:
     grpc_service:
       envoy_grpc:
         cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster
     disable_clear_route_cache: true
   ```

2. **Update to v1.31**:
   ```yaml
   # v1.31 configuration - Option A: Use new route_cache_action field
   ext_proc:
     grpc_service:
       envoy_grpc:
         cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster
     route_cache_action: RETAIN  # Replaces disable_clear_route_cache: true
   ```

3. **Backward Compatibility**:
   - The old `disable_clear_route_cache: true` setting is automatically converted to `route_cache_action: RETAIN`
   - The old `disable_clear_route_cache: false` (or omitted) maps to `route_cache_action: DEFAULT`

**Validation Errors:**
```
Configuration Error: "disable_clear_route_cache and route_cache_action can not
be set to none-default at the same time."
```

This error occurs when both are specified with non-default values. **Remove the old field and use the new one instead.**

**Best Practice:**
- Always use `route_cache_action` in new configurations
- Only use `disable_clear_route_cache` if you must maintain v1.30 compatibility

---

## Deprecated Features

### 1. `async_mode` Field (Already Unimplemented)

**Status**: Deprecated in favor of `observability_mode`

**Background:**
- `async_mode` was reserved in v1.30 as `reserved 4; reserved "async_mode";`
- It was never implemented and marked with `[#not-implemented-hide:]`

**What's Changing:**
- v1.31 introduces `observability_mode` as the official mechanism for non-blocking external processor communication
- `observability_mode` supersedes the planned (but never implemented) `async_mode`

**Migration Path:**
- If you have any references to `async_mode`, ignore them or remove them
- Use `observability_mode` instead for "send and go" communication patterns

---

## New Features

### 1. Route Cache Action Enum

**Purpose**: Provides explicit control over when route caches are cleared based on external processor responses.

**Field Details:**
- Field Number: 18 (in ExternalProcessor message)
- Type: Enum (ExternalProcessor.RouteCacheAction)
- Default: DEFAULT
- Applies to: Request header processing responses

**Enum Values:**

| Value | Behavior | Use Case |
|-------|----------|----------|
| **DEFAULT** | Clear route cache only when the external processor explicitly sets `clear_route_cache: true` in the response | Normal operation - processor controls cache invalidation |
| **CLEAR** | Always clear the route cache regardless of processor response | When header mutations might affect routing decisions; ensures fresh route computation |
| **RETAIN** | Never clear the route cache regardless of processor response | When processor modifications don't affect routing; improves performance |

**Configuration Example:**

```yaml
# Force route cache clearing on header mutations
ext_proc:
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster
  route_cache_action: CLEAR

# Or use the legacy form that auto-converts
ext_proc:
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster
  disable_clear_route_cache: true  # Maps to RETAIN
```

**Behavioral Details:**
- Route cache action is evaluated after the external processor responds to request headers
- The action only applies to header processing responses, not body or trailer responses
- Setting this field overrides any `clear_route_cache` field in the processor response (when not DEFAULT)

**Implementation Location:**
- Source: `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/ext_proc.cc` (lines 186-187, 213-221)
- Proto: `api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/ext_proc.proto` (lines 232-258)

---

### 2. Observability Mode

**Purpose**: Enables "Send and Go" mode where the external processor observes traffic without blocking request/response processing.

**Field Details:**
- Field Number: 17 (in ExternalProcessor message)
- Type: Boolean
- Default: false
- Proto Location: `api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/ext_proc.proto` (lines 221-243)

**Configuration Example:**

```yaml
ext_proc:
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster
  observability_mode: true
  deferred_close_timeout: 5000ms  # Optional: defaults to 5 seconds
```

**Behavior When Enabled:**

1. **Non-Blocking Communication**: Each part of the HTTP request or response specified by `ProcessingMode` is sent to the external processor without pausing filter chain iteration
2. **No Waiting for Responses**: Envoy doesn't wait for responses from the external processor; any responses received are ignored
3. **Stream Closure**: The external processor can still close the stream to indicate it needs no more messages
4. **Limited Body Processing**: Only STREAMED body processing mode is fully supported; other body modes (BUFFERED, BUFFERED_PARTIAL) will be ignored, but NONE mode (skip body) works as expected
5. **Timeout Management**: The `message_timeout` field doesn't apply in observability mode since responses aren't awaited

**Important Constraints:**

- Ō£ģ Request headers will be sent in observability mode
- Ō£ģ Response headers will be sent in observability mode
- Ō£ģ Request/response bodies will be sent in STREAMED mode without waiting
- Ō£ģ Trailers will be sent if configured
- Ō£ģ The external processor can close the stream at any time
- ŌØī The external processor must NOT send back responses (they'll be ignored)
- ŌØī Header mutations from processor responses cannot be applied (no responses expected)
- ŌØī Response modifications are not possible (body/header/trailer mutations cannot be applied)

**Use Cases:**

```yaml
# Observability use case 1: Traffic monitoring
ext_proc:
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: monitoring_service
  observability_mode: true
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
    response_header_mode: SEND
    request_body_mode: STREAMED
    response_body_mode: STREAMED

# Observability use case 2: Non-blocking audit logging
ext_proc:
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: audit_service
  observability_mode: true
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
    response_header_mode: SEND
```

**Flow Control Warning:**

ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ **Important**: Flow control mechanisms to prevent fast senders from overwhelming the slow external processor are still under development (tracked in GitHub issue #33319). Use observability mode with caution in high-throughput environments and consider:
- Implementing backpressure at the processor
- Monitoring processor queue depths
- Using circuit breakers if processor falls behind

**Implementation Location:**
- Source: `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/ext_proc.cc` (lines 186, 430, 657, 683, 738, 881)
- Proto: `api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/ext_proc.proto` (lines 221-243)

---

### 3. Deferred Close Timeout

**Purpose**: Controls how long gRPC streams remain open after the HTTP stream completes in observability mode.

**Field Details:**
- Field Number: 19 (in ExternalProcessor message)
- Type: google.protobuf.Duration
- Default: 5000ms (5 seconds)
- Valid Range: 0ms to 3600s
- Proto Location: `api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/ext_proc.proto` (lines 260-265)

**Configuration Example:**

```yaml
ext_proc:
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster
  observability_mode: true
  deferred_close_timeout: 3s        # 3 seconds
  # OR
  deferred_close_timeout: 10000ms   # 10 seconds
```

**Behavior:**

1. **When observability mode is enabled**, gRPC streams may remain open longer than the HTTP stream lifetime
2. **Deferred closure**: When the HTTP request/response completes, Envoy doesn't immediately close the gRPC stream
3. **Timeout application**: Instead, Envoy sets a timer that will close the stream after `deferred_close_timeout` expires
4. **Early closure**: The external processor can close the stream earlier by closing its end
5. **Processing window**: The external processor has `deferred_close_timeout` duration to finish processing any buffered messages

**Use Cases:**

```yaml
# Short timeout for low-latency services
ext_proc:
  observability_mode: true
  deferred_close_timeout: 100ms

# Long timeout for batch processors
ext_proc:
  observability_mode: true
  deferred_close_timeout: 30s

# Match to external processor processing SLA
ext_proc:
  observability_mode: true
  deferred_close_timeout: 5s
```

**Implementation Details:**
- Default constant: `DEFAULT_DEFERRED_CLOSE_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000` (source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/ext_proc.h:150)
- Managed by: `ThreadLocalStreamManager` class for per-stream lifecycle management
- Triggered in: `DeferredDeletableStream::deferredClose()` method

---

## Configuration Updates

### Complete Configuration Example: v1.30 ŌåÆ v1.31

**v1.30 Configuration:**

```yaml
http_filters:
- name: envoy.filters.http.ext_proc
  typed_config:
    "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor
    grpc_service:
      envoy_grpc:
        cluster_name: ext_proc_service
    processing_mode:
      request_header_mode: SEND
      response_header_mode: SEND
      request_body_mode: BUFFERED
    failure_mode_allow: false
    message_timeout: 200ms
    disable_clear_route_cache: true
    stat_prefix: "my_ext_proc_"
```

**v1.31 Configuration (Recommended):**

```yaml
http_filters:
- name: envoy.filters.http.ext_proc
  typed_config:
    "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor
    grpc_service:
      envoy_grpc:
        cluster_name: ext_proc_service
    processing_mode:
      request_header_mode: SEND
      response_header_mode: SEND
      request_body_mode: BUFFERED
    failure_mode_allow: false
    message_timeout: 200ms
    route_cache_action: RETAIN          # Changed from disable_clear_route_cache
    stat_prefix: "my_ext_proc_"
    # Optional: new features in v1.31
    # observability_mode: false         # Defaults to false (blocking mode)
    # deferred_close_timeout: 5000ms    # Defaults to 5 seconds
```

**v1.31 Configuration (with Observability Mode):**

```yaml
http_filters:
- name: envoy.filters.http.ext_proc
  typed_config:
    "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor
    grpc_service:
      envoy_grpc:
        cluster_name: ext_proc_service
    processing_mode:
      request_header_mode: SEND
      response_header_mode: SEND
      request_body_mode: STREAMED       # STREAMED when using observability mode
    failure_mode_allow: false
    observability_mode: true             # Enable observability/audit mode
    deferred_close_timeout: 3s            # Custom timeout
    stat_prefix: "audit_proc_"
```

---

## Behavioral Changes

### 1. Timeout Error Status Code

**Change**: Timeout errors in external processor now return **504 Gateway Timeout** instead of **500 Internal Server Error**.

**Impact**:
- Clients can distinguish between ext_proc timeouts and other server errors
- Load balancers can handle 504 differently (retry, failover, etc.)
- Better observability and error tracking

**Example:**

```
v1.30 Behavior: ext_proc timeout ŌåÆ 500 Internal Server Error
v1.31 Behavior: ext_proc timeout ŌåÆ 504 Gateway Timeout
```

**Affected Scenarios:**
- Request headers timeout while waiting for processor response
- Request body timeout
- Response headers timeout
- Response body timeout

**Log/Metric Changes:**
- The metric `ext_proc.message_timeouts` counter remains the same
- HTTP status code in access logs will change to 504

---

### 2. gRPC Tracing Context Propagation

**Change** (Minor): Tracing context headers are now properly propagated in bidirectional gRPC streams.

**Context**: Both Envoy gRPC and Google C++ gRPC clients now propagate tracing context when calling external services like ext_proc.

**Impact on ext_proc**:
- Tracing spans from the external processor request can now be correlated with the main request trace
- Better observability when using distributed tracing
- External processor traces will show up in trace collection systems

---

## Migration Steps

### Step 1: Review Current Configuration

Check if your configuration uses `disable_clear_route_cache`:

```bash
# Search for disable_clear_route_cache in your config
grep -r "disable_clear_route_cache" /path/to/configs/
```

### Step 2: Update Route Cache Management

**If using `disable_clear_route_cache: true`:**
```diff
- disable_clear_route_cache: true
+ route_cache_action: RETAIN
```

**If using `disable_clear_route_cache: false` or omitting it:**
```yaml
# No change needed, but explicitly setting is clearer:
route_cache_action: DEFAULT
```

### Step 3: Plan for New Features

Decide if your use case benefits from the new features:

- **Observability Mode**: Do you have monitoring/audit requirements? Enable with `observability_mode: true`
- **Timeout Handling**: Are your external processors fast? Consider reducing `deferred_close_timeout`
- **Explicit Route Cache Control**: Should you use CLEAR instead of relying on processor responses?

### Step 4: Test Migration

```yaml
# Test configuration with new field
ext_proc:
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster
  route_cache_action: RETAIN     # New field
  # Keep other settings same
```

**Validation Checklist:**
- [ ] Configuration loads without errors
- [ ] HTTP requests flow through ext_proc normally
- [ ] Route caching behavior matches expectations
- [ ] Metrics and logs show expected behavior
- [ ] Error responses have correct status codes

### Step 5: Deploy to Staging

- Deploy v1.31 to a staging environment
- Run integration tests
- Verify with production-like traffic patterns
- Monitor error rates and latency

### Step 6: Production Rollout

- Roll out gradually using canary deployments
- Monitor metrics:
  - `ext_proc.message_timeouts` (should match v1.30)
  - HTTP 504 responses (previously 500)
  - Route cache invalidation rate
- Have rollback plan ready

---

## Testing Recommendations

### 1. Configuration Validation Tests

```yaml
# Test 1: Verify route_cache_action: CLEAR works
test_config:
  route_cache_action: CLEAR
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
expected_behavior: "Route cache cleared on header mutation"

# Test 2: Verify route_cache_action: RETAIN works
test_config:
  route_cache_action: RETAIN
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
expected_behavior: "Route cache never cleared"

# Test 3: Verify error on conflicting config
test_config:
  disable_clear_route_cache: true
  route_cache_action: CLEAR
expected_behavior: "Configuration error thrown"
```

### 2. Observability Mode Tests

```yaml
# Test 4: Request/response streaming without blocking
test_config:
  observability_mode: true
  processing_mode:
    request_body_mode: STREAMED
    response_body_mode: STREAMED
expected_behavior: |
  - Filter doesn't wait for processor responses
  - Request completes even if processor slow
  - Processor messages sent asynchronously

# Test 5: Deferred closure timeout
test_config:
  observability_mode: true
  deferred_close_timeout: 1s
expected_behavior: |
  - Stream closes after 1 second
  - Processor has 1 second to finish
  - No messages sent after timeout
```

### 3. Error Handling Tests

```yaml
# Test 6: Timeout returns 504
test_config:
  message_timeout: 100ms
  grpc_service: # Simulated slow service
expected_behavior: "HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout returned"

# Test 7: Observability mode ignores responses
test_config:
  observability_mode: true
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
expected_behavior: |
  - Processor response mutations ignored
  - Stream continues normally
  - No header modifications applied
```

### 4. Integration Test Scenario

```python
# Pseudo-test code for integration testing
def test_ext_proc_v131_migration():
    # Start v1.31 Envoy with new config
    envoy = start_envoy(config_v131)

    # Test 1: Normal operation unchanged
    assert make_request("/api").status == 200

    # Test 2: Route cache action works
    assert route_cache_cleared() == False  # With RETAIN

    # Test 3: Observability mode non-blocking
    with slow_processor(delay=5s):
        start_time = time()
        response = make_request("/api")
        elapsed = time() - start_time
        assert elapsed < 1s  # Didn't wait for processor
        assert response.status == 200

    # Test 4: Timeout returns 504
    with failing_processor():
        response = make_request("/api", timeout=1s)
        assert response.status == 504
```

---

## Rollback Guidance

### Rollback to v1.30

If you encounter issues with v1.31, here's how to safely rollback:

### Step 1: Prepare Fallback Configuration

Keep a v1.30 compatible configuration ready:

```yaml
# v1.30 compatible configuration
ext_proc:
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
    response_header_mode: SEND
  disable_clear_route_cache: true  # Use old field name
  message_timeout: 200ms
```

### Step 2: Identify Issues

Check logs for error patterns:

```bash
# Configuration validation errors
grep "disable_clear_route_cache and route_cache_action" envoy.log

# Timeout errors (now 504 instead of 500)
grep "504 Gateway Timeout" envoy_access.log

# Observability mode issues (if enabled)
grep "observability_mode" envoy.log | grep ERROR
```

### Step 3: Rollback Procedure

1. **Update configuration** to use old `disable_clear_route_cache` field
2. **Redeploy Envoy v1.30** or pin to v1.31 with backward-compatible config
3. **Verify traffic** flows normally
4. **Monitor metrics** to ensure stability

### Step 4: Prevent Recurrence

After rollback:
1. Identify root cause
2. File issues if bugs found
3. Plan retry after fixes
4. Test more thoroughly in staging

### Critical Rollback Indicators

```
Immediate rollback if:
- Configuration won't load with "cannot be set to none-default at the same time" error
- 504 responses breaking downstream systems (incompatible with 500)
- Observability mode preventing normal traffic flow
- gRPC connection failures to ext_proc
```

---

## Common Issues and Solutions

### Issue 1: Configuration Validation Error

**Error Message:**
```
"disable_clear_route_cache and route_cache_action can not be set to none-default at the same time."
```

**Cause**: Both fields set with non-default values

**Solution**:
```yaml
# Remove the old field
- disable_clear_route_cache: true
+ route_cache_action: RETAIN

# Or use only one field
# Use route_cache_action: DEFAULT  (or omit it)
# And remove disable_clear_route_cache
```

### Issue 2: Route Cache Not Clearing When Expected

**Symptom**: Routes aren't being recalculated after header changes

**Diagnosis**:
```
Check setting:
- route_cache_action: RETAIN ŌåÆ Cache never clears (expected)
- route_cache_action: DEFAULT ŌåÆ Cache clears only if processor sets clear_route_cache
- route_cache_action: CLEAR ŌåÆ Cache always clears (ensure this is used)
```

**Solution**:
```yaml
# If header mutations should affect routing
route_cache_action: CLEAR

# If you want processor to control cache clearing
route_cache_action: DEFAULT
# AND processor must set clear_route_cache: true in response
```

### Issue 3: Observability Mode Not Sending Messages

**Symptom**: Processor doesn't receive request/response messages

**Diagnosis**:
```
Check:
1. observability_mode: true is set
2. processing_mode has appropriate send modes (not all SKIP)
3. deferred_close_timeout is reasonable
```

**Solution**:
```yaml
ext_proc:
  observability_mode: true
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND      # Must be SEND
    response_header_mode: SEND     # Must be SEND
    request_body_mode: STREAMED    # Use STREAMED, not NONE
    response_body_mode: STREAMED
```

### Issue 4: Observability Mode Responses Being Ignored

**Symptom**: Processor responses causing mutations but nothing happens

**Expected Behavior**:
```
In observability mode:
- Processor responses are IGNORED
- No headers/body/trailer mutations applied
- No mode_override processed
- No immediate_response sent
```

**Solution**:
Use observability mode ONLY for monitoring/audit. For actual request modification:
```yaml
# For modifying requests/responses
ext_proc:
  observability_mode: false        # Use blocking mode
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
    response_header_mode: SEND
  message_timeout: 1s
```

### Issue 5: Timeout Still Returns 500 Instead of 504

**Cause**: Using v1.30 behavior expectation

**Check**:
```
Verify Envoy version is actually v1.31:
$ envoy --version
```

**Verify Status Code**:
```yaml
ext_proc:
  message_timeout: 100ms
  grpc_service:
    envoy_grpc:
      cluster_name: slow_service

# With slow service:
# v1.30: Returns 500 Internal Server Error
# v1.31: Returns 504 Gateway Timeout
```

**Solution**: Update downstream error handling to expect 504:
```python
# Before v1.31
if status == 500:  # Could be ext_proc timeout
    retry_with_backoff()

# After v1.31
if status == 504:  # Gateway Timeout (ext_proc specific)
    retry_with_backoff()
if status == 500:  # Other server errors
    alert_ops()
```

---

## Field Reference

### ExternalProcessor Message Fields (v1.31)

| Field | Number | Type | Default | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|------|---------|--------|-------|
| `grpc_service` | 1 | GrpcService | - | Required | External processor service config |
| `failure_mode_allow` | 2 | bool | false | Active | Continue on processor failure |
| `processing_mode` | 3 | ProcessingMode | - | Active | Which parts to send |
| `request_attributes` | 5 | string[] | [] | Active | Attributes to populate in request |
| `response_attributes` | 6 | string[] | [] | Active | Attributes to populate in response |
| `message_timeout` | 7 | Duration | 200ms | Active | Per-message timeout |
| `stat_prefix` | 8 | string | "" | Active | Statistics prefix |
| `mutation_rules` | 9 | HeaderMutationRules | - | Active | What headers can be mutated |
| `max_message_timeout` | 10 | Duration | 0 | Active | Max override_message_timeout |
| `disable_clear_route_cache` | 11 | bool | false | Deprecated | Use route_cache_action instead |
| `forward_rules` | 12 | HeaderForwardingRules | - | Active | Which headers to forward |
| `filter_metadata` | 13 | Struct | {} | Active | Filter state metadata |
| `allow_mode_override` | 14 | bool | false | Active | Allow response to override mode |
| `disable_immediate_response` | 15 | bool | false | Active | Disable immediate_response |
| `metadata_options` | 16 | MetadataOptions | - | Active | Dynamic metadata handling |
| `observability_mode` | 17 | bool | false | New | Non-blocking observation mode |
| `route_cache_action` | 18 | RouteCacheAction | DEFAULT | New | Route cache management |
| `deferred_close_timeout` | 19 | Duration | 5s | New | gRPC stream close timeout |

---

## Version Compatibility Matrix

| Version | Release Date | status | Notes |
|---------|---|---|---|
| v1.30.0 | April 2024 | EOL Support | `disable_clear_route_cache` only |
| v1.30.1-4 | 2024 | EOL Support | Bug fixes for v1.30 |
| v1.31.0 | July 2024 | LTS | New route_cache_action, observability_mode |

**Upgrade Path**:
```
v1.30.x ŌåÆ v1.31.0 (requires config changes for route cache)
         ŌåÆ v1.31+ (continue with new features)
```

---

## API Proto Files Changed

### Modified Files:

1. **api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/ext_proc.proto**
   - Added `RouteCacheAction` enum (lines 105-120)
   - Updated next-free-field from 18 to 20
   - Added new import: `udpa/annotations/migrate.proto`
   - Modified `disable_clear_route_cache` field 11 with migration annotation
   - Added `route_cache_action` field 18
   - Added `deferred_close_timeout` field 19
   - Modified `observability_mode` documentation

2. **api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/processing_mode.proto**
   - No changes (identical in v1.30 and v1.31)

### Unchanged Proto Files:

- Service definitions remain in `envoy/service/ext_proc/v3/`
- All request/response message structures unchanged
- ProcessingMode and HeaderSendMode enums unchanged

---

## Best Practices for v1.31

### 1. Use route_cache_action Instead of disable_clear_route_cache

```yaml
# Ō£ģ GOOD - v1.31 best practice
route_cache_action: CLEAR    # Explicit intent

# ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ ACCEPTABLE - still works but deprecated
disable_clear_route_cache: true

# ŌØī BAD - both set
disable_clear_route_cache: true
route_cache_action: CLEAR
```

### 2. Choose Right Mode for Your Use Case

```yaml
# Ō£ģ Use BLOCKING MODE for modifications
ext_proc:
  observability_mode: false
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
    response_header_mode: SEND
  message_timeout: 1s
  # Processor can modify headers/body

# Ō£ģ Use OBSERVABILITY MODE for monitoring
ext_proc:
  observability_mode: true
  processing_mode:
    request_header_mode: SEND
    response_header_mode: SEND
    request_body_mode: STREAMED
  deferred_close_timeout: 5s
  # Processor cannot modify, only observe

# ŌØī WRONG - blocking mode with huge timeout
message_timeout: 60s
# This blocks request for 60 seconds!

# Ō£ģ CORRECT - reasonable timeout
message_timeout: 1s  # 1 second max per message
```

### 3. Monitor the Right Metrics

```
Key metrics to track:

1. ext_proc.message_timeouts
   - Should not increase significantly in v1.31
   - Check if set to appropriate duration

2. HTTP status 504 responses (new in v1.31)
   - Count how many are from ext_proc
   - Should be low (implies good processor latency)

3. ext_proc.streams_started and streams_closed
   - In observability mode: may differ timing
   - In blocking mode: should be equal over time

4. Route cache statistics
   - Monitor impact of route_cache_action setting
   - CLEAR = more route recomputation
   - RETAIN = more route cache hits
```

### 4. Error Handling

```python
# Handle 504 Gateway Timeout as distinct from 500
def handle_response(response):
    if response.status == 504:
        # External processor timeout
        logger.info("ext_proc timeout, may retry")
        return retry_with_backoff(response)
    elif response.status == 500:
        # Other server error
        logger.error("Server error, escalate")
        return alert_on_call()
    else:
        return handle_normally(response)
```

---

## Support and Resources

### Documentation Links:
- [Envoy v1.31 Release Notes](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/intro/version_history/v1.31/v1.31.0)

### GitHub Issues:
